$comment: DRAFT v1.6.0 boundary-stress non-regression FLOORS gate (the per-locale half; the 4-shape TARGET half is scripts/eval/boundary-stress-gate.ts). Derived from v4.4.0-boundary.json (the last-shipped contract, active through v4.6.0). Per the no-silent-gate-drift discipline EVERY deviation is stated here, not edited quietly. STATED CHANGES vs v4.4.0-boundary: (1) fr.house_number 91.0 -> 87.0 — the v4.6.0 documented re-baseline (the recovery plateaus ~87.4 at the reordered-case SOTA frontier; 91 predates it and is unachievable). (2) us.locality 62.2 -> 72.9 — TIGHTENED to the recipe's stated floor. Rationale: this run's headline lever (the boundary shard) is exactly the kind of data-mix change that can pull capacity off the spine — we OBSERVED a mid-training locality dip (0.684->0.606) before it recovered — so the floor must be tight enough to catch a locality regression at ship; v1.5.x scored locality 72.9+ on this eval, so 72.9 is both achievable and protective.
$comment_us_street: FLAGGED FOR OPERATOR: the recipe (v1.6.0-boundary-stress.yaml) states 'US street >=80.4', but the ledger shows 80.4 is the PRE-#492 shipped value — recent models sit at the #492 stability ceiling (us.street 76.2 / 75.5 / 77.9 across v1.5.x). The committed v4.4.0 floor is 74.0. Kept at 74.0 here (defensible: the actual last-shipped non-regression floor). The proper fix is to re-anchor to v1.5.1's MEASURED us.street (run per-locale-f1.ts on the v1.5.1 baseline) and floor a hair below it. Do NOT encode 80.4 — it auto-fails a healthy model.
label: v1.6.0-boundary-stress
